data.title: Mortgage rates move up to reach year-ago levels ahead of the holiday season
data.description:
Mortgage rates moved up to reach a similar level as in the year-ago period ahead of the holiday season, the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.72% as of December 19, up from 6.60% last week and 6.67% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.92%, compared to 5.84% a week ago and 5.95% in the prior-year period.

"For the most part, mortgage rates have moved between 6 and 7 percent over the last 12 months," said Freddie Mac's chief economist, Sam Khater.

"Homebuyers are slowly digesting these higher rates and are gradually willing to move forward with buying a home, resulting in additional purchase activity," added Khater.

data.title: El Salvador to sell or discontinue its bitcoin wallet as part of IMF deal
data.description:
El Salvador will sell or discontinue its bitcoin (BTC-USD) wallet Chivo as part of a new deal with the International Monetary Fund, an official said on Thursday.

El Salvador, the first country to make bitcoin (BTC-USD) legal tender, agreed to scale back on its bitcoin-related policies. Under the $1.4B loan deal, the nation will allow businesses to voluntarily accept bitcoin as a means of payment, while ensuring that taxes will continue to be paid exclusively in U.S. dollars, rather than in BTC.

For the public sector, participation in bitcoin-related activities, as well as transactions and purchases involving bitcoin, will be restricted, the IMF said in a statement.

The move is perhaps part of the reason for bitcoin's (BTC-USD) weakness Thursday, sliding 4.5% to roughly $100K.

Still, the Central American country, where bitcoin (BTC-USD) remains -- and will continue to be -- legal tender, will keep adding bitcoin to its reserves "at possibly an accelerated pace," Stacy Herbert, director of the national bitcoin office, wrote in a post on X.

She noted the Chivo wallet will be "sold or wound down but the many private sector bitcoin wallets will continue servicing El Salvador.

data.title: Big Lots looks for a lifeline as going out of business sales ramp up
data.description:
Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) announced on Thursday that it does not anticipate completing its previously announced asset purchase agreement with Nexus Capital Management.

The retailer said it continues to work toward completing an alternative going concern transaction with Nexus or another party, with a goal to complete a sale by early January.

In addition, Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) is preparing to start all going out of business sales at all remaining stores in the coming days to protect the value of its estate. The company believes that the going out of business sales will not preclude it from effectuating a going concern transaction.

In the Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) bankruptcy filing, the company listed its assets and liabilities in the range of $1 billion to $10 billion and showed creditors in the range of 5,001 to 10,000 parties.

data.title: Wall Street bounces back after worst day since early August as traders digest hawkish Fed
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday staged a rebound after suffering its worst session in over three months. Market participants took some heart from data that showed strong U.S. economic growth, after having a night to mull over the Federal Reserve's hawkish interest rate cut.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.51% at 5,901.94 points in midday trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.65% at 19,519.42 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.47% at 42,525.58 points.

Of the 11 S&P sectors, six were in the green, led by Technology. Health Care topped the losers.

Over in the fixed-income markets, Treasury yields were mixed. Longer-end maturities climbed, with the 10-year yield (US10Y) up 5 basis points to 4.57%. Shorter-end instruments slipped, with the 2-year yield (US2Y) down 6 basis points to 4.31%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

The Fed on Wednesday delivered a 25 basis point rate cut, as widely expected. But it was the central bank's updated Summary of Economic Projections, or dot plot, that was the story of the day. The Fed now sees higher inflation and fewer rate cuts in 2025, with chair Jerome Powell stressing caution going forward.

Markets reacted in an ugly manner, with the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) eventually sinking more than 3%.

The mood was much calmer on Thursday, and it was helped by a positive economic calendar.

Before the opening bell, the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis said real gross domestic product (GDP) improved at an annual rate of 3.1% in Q3 2024, a higher reading than the previous estimate of +2.8%. The update indicated that the U.S. economy remained robust, but was also another reminder that perhaps more easing from the Fed is not immediately required.

"GDP remains well above the pre-pandemic forecast. This reflects a combination of productivity being as expected and the labor force being much larger than expected - mostly due to immigration," Jason Furman, former deputy director of the U.S. National Economic Council, said on X (formerly Twitter).

"Overall (it) is hard to understand why the Fed cut rates yesterday in an economy growing at a 3% annual rate," Furman added.

At the same time as the GDP estimate, data from the U.S. Department of Labor showed that the number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week fell more than expected.

Also, in a notable development, the Conference Board's U.S. leading indicator index rose in November for the first time since February 2022. The index has long been seen as a recession bellwether, however its divergence over the last two years or so from a largely healthy economy had called that status into question.

Turning to Thursday's active stocks, Micron Technology (MU) slumped -16.3% and was the top percentage loser on the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND). The chipmaker's current quarter guidance disappointed investors, and so did its quarterly revenue decline in NAND flash memory.

The world's largest shoe company, Nike (NKE), and global economic bellwether FedEx (FDX) will be reporting results after the closing bell.

More on the markets

  • The Fed Fumble And The Dollar Wrecking Ball
  • Did The Fed Just Trigger A Correction?
  • Stocks Have Swung From Overbought To Oversold Seemingly Overnight
  • Despite Fed's hawkish tone, Wells Fargo forecasts 'solid growth in 2025'
  • 25 U.S. ‘highest conviction’ stocks – UBS

data.title: FactSet Q1 earnings top consensus as ASV climbs, margins hold up, guidance affirmed
data.description:
FactSet (NYSE:FDS) stock rose 2.6% in Thursday midday trading after the financial data company posted better-than-expected earnings and revenue in the first quarter of its fiscal 2025.

The company reaffirmed its FY2025 guidance, including GAAP revenue of $2.29B-$2.31B (consensus $2.30B), adjusted operating margin of 36.0%-37.0%, and adjusted EPS of $16.80-$17.40 (consensus $17.18).

Q1 adjusted EPS of $4.37, topping the average analyst estimate of $4.28, rose from $3.74 in Q4 and $4.12 in last year's Q1. The beat was driven by better margins and a slightly lower tax rate, said Evercore ISI analyst David Motemaden.

GAAP revenue of $568.7M for the quarter ended Nov. 30, 2024 beat the $565.1M consensus and climbed from $562.2M in the previous quarter and $542.2M a year ago. Organic revenue rose 4.7% Y/Y to $567.7M, driven by wealth, asset owners and institutional asset managers.

Annual subscription value ("ASV") was $2.27B at Nov. 30, 2024, vs. $2.16B at Nov. 30, 2023. Organic ASV was $2.26B at the end of Q1 2025, up $98.1M from the previous year and up $3.4M over the last three months.

Adjusted operating margin of 37.6% improved from 35.8% in the prior quarter and was flat vs. a year ago.

EBITDA of $229.9M increased 5.0% from $219.0M in Q1 2024.

"While we didn’t see any glaring signs of green shoots in the result, we are encouraged by the increase in client retention to 91% from 90% where it has been running over the last year," Evercore ISI's Motemaden wrote in a note after the earnings release came out.

Earlier, FactSet Research Systems Non-GAAP EPS of $4.37 beats by $0.09, revenue of $568.7M beats by $3.65M

data.title: UBS’s global equity focus list: stocks within Green-tech
data.description:
UBS analysts selected their top stocks from four technology subsectors – the following are from the “green” tech group.

“Many of [technology’s] subsectors are seen as a leading indicator of innovation and societal progress today, such as mobile tech’s role in helping emerging nations leapfrog the PC era, the rise of electric vehicles as a better environmental choice, and cloud tech that helps enterprises process big data efficiently,” said Hartmut Issel, head of Equity and Credit APAC and CIO at UBS.

“Greentech” is one of the leading disruptors undergoing technological transformation and is expected to create consequential and enduring impact, he said.

The following five stocks are attractive opportunities in the short- to medium-term. These are companies that would benefit from fiscal spending and tech innovations.

“We see industry leaders for 2030 emerging from these sectors,” said Issel. The following stocks are expected “to deliver superior earnings growth to the market, boosted by positive, durable structural trends supporting the theme.”

UBS also named the leading stocks in enabling tech, fin-tech, and health tech.

Greentech stocks:

  • L’Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) – “The firm is a relatively defensive name for a chemical company and is well hedged against inflation in its onsite gas business (30% of sales),” analysts said. “The stock is trading at a discount to its global peers in terms of forward P/E valuation.”
  • Enel SpA (OTCPK:ENLAY) – “Enel trades at a material discount to peers and its historical valuation. The planned investments in renewables and networks should lead to very attractive earnings and dividend growth in the coming years.”
  • Linde plc (LIN) – “Resilient demand and focus on cost optimization through the cycle should support double-digit EPS growth in the core business,” analysts said.
  • Rolls-Royce Holdings (OTCPK:RYCEY) – “[Its] Power division is gaining increasing exposure to the high-growth segment of data centers… [This is] another robust growth driver for the medium term.”
  • Trane Technologies (TT) – The company “benefits from the secular decarbonization trend in the US.” It is also “supported by solid demand [with] a healthy backlog, and resilient pricing that provides strong visibility for Trane's revenues and margins as supply chain constraints ease.”

data.title: Activist Barington nominates three for Matthews International board
data.description:
Activist investor Barington Capital formally nominated three for casket-maker Matthews International's (NASDAQ:MATW) board.

Barington, which has a ~2% stake in Matthews (MATW), is nominating Ana B. Amicarella, Chan W. Galbato and James Mitarotonda, to the board, according to a statement on Thursday. The investor filed a preliminary proxy statement.

The proxy filing comes after the investor last Tuesday called for the casket-maker to replace its CEO, sell off underperforming businesses and announced plans to nominate three board directors. Barington has been an investor in Matthews since 2022 and the hedge fund pushed for a refreshed board in December of that year.

Matthews, in response to Barington, last Tuesday, highlighted that when it announced in its most recent results it hired JPMorgan to support the evaluation of potential strategic alternatives for its portfolio of businesses.

"Now that the agreement with Barington has expired, rather than engage in a private dialogue with our board and potentially exploring a constructive path forward, Barington has decided to wage a costly and disruptive proxy contest," Matthew said in a statement last Tuesday.

data.title: What to expect from Keurig Dr Pepper in 2025
data.description:
Analysts generally expect Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) to have a solid performance in 2025, with expectations for revenue growth of 4.4% and earnings per share growth of 6.3%. Those projects align with the company's long-term financial algorithm, which aims for consistent mid-single-digit net sales growth.

The consensus Wall Street rating on the beverage stock is at Buy, with UBS being particularly bullish. Notably, Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) was named as one of UBS' 25 highest conviction picks for 2025, with the setup called increasingly attractive.

Analyst Joseph Parkhill and his team said that in an environment where top and bottom-line visibility is very limited across the group, Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) stands out because Street estimates do not appear to be at risk.

"The coffee category will certainly remain in focus, but we believe that an acceleration in organic sales growth from 3Q levels (thanks to partnership benefits + the addition of Ghost) provides ample flexibility across the P&L for KDP to deliver on its LT algo even if the at-home coffee category remains more subdued (with Str. estimates already calling for only modest +LSD growth in US Coffee)," read the UBS deep dive.

The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on KDP is more cautious at a Hold rating and a score that ranks 9th out of 16 beverage stocks.

Shares of Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) are -2.4% on a year-to-date basis. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 2.83%.

data.title: Medline files for IPO, valuation could hit $50B: report
data.description:
Medline, a leading provider of medical supplies, has confidentially filed for an initial public offering that could value the company as much as $50B, although the size of the deal has yet to be determined, according to Reuters.

Reuters reported last month that Medline could aim to raise over $5B, which would make it one of the largest IPOs of the past few years.

Medline was sold by the Mills family in 2021 to Blackstone (BX), The Carlyle Group (CG) and Hellman & Friedman for $34B. Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC Pte also reportedly holds a stake in the company, which sells such medical staples as surgical gloves, gowns, masks and lab supplies.
